task_prompt:

# Question
Which of the following is the only complete cailage ring in the respiratory tree?

# Options
A) Cricoid cailage
B) Thyroid cailage
C) Cunieform cailage
D) Epiglottis
prompt:

# Question
Which of the following is the only complete cailage ring in the respiratory tree?

# Options
A) Cricoid cailage
B) Thyroid cailage
C) Cunieform cailage
D) Epiglottis

This is attempt 1 of 5. If this attempt does not pass, you will receive feedback and can revise it on the remaining attempts.
output:
The cricoid cartilage is the only complete ring of cartilage in the respiratory tract. All other cartilages (thyroid, tracheal rings, bronchial cartilages) are incomplete (C-shaped).

Answer: A
